headline: Eli Lilly Announces $7 Billion Deal. What It Means for the Stock.
summary: Eli Lilly  announced Monday it will acquire Boston-based Kelonia Therapeutics with an initial payment of $3.25 billion, as the pharma giant looks to bolster its position in the global cancer drug market.  Eli Lilly stock ticked 0.3% lower to $924.10 a share at the opening bell on Monday.  In the deal, Kelonia shareholders will receive up to $7 billion in cash, which will include an upfront payment of $3.25 billion, according to Eli Lilly.

headline: Eli Lilly Strikes Deal for Cancer Biotech
summary: Eli Lilly struck a deal to acquire Kelonia Therapeutics for $3.25 billion, the companies said Monday.  The deal price could reach as much as $7 billion if Kelonia reaches certain clinical, regulatory and commercial milestones, they said.  The Wall Street Journal reported Sunday the two companies were near such a deal.

The Wall Street Journal reported Sunday the two companies were near such a deal.

headline: Eli Lilly Closes in on a $2 Billion Deal. What It Means for the Stock.
summary: Eli Lilly  is in advanced talks to acquire Boston-based Kelonia Therapeutics for more than $2 billion, according to The Wall Street Journal, citing people familiar with the matter.  Pharma giant Eli Lilly was trading 0.3% down in premarket trading Monday, at $924.03 a share.  Privately-owned Kelonia is developing novel treatments for multiple myeloma, a blood cancer.

headline: Is It Too Late To Consider Eli Lilly (LLY) After Strong Multi Year Share Price Gains
summary: This article considers whether Eli Lilly, at around US$927 per share, is still priced for opportunity or already reflects high expectations. It walks through what the current market price might be implying about the stock. The share price has seen mixed moves recently, with a 1.3% decline over the last week, a 2.2% gain over the past month, a 14.2% year to date decline, an 11.2% return over 1 year, a 146.0% return over 3 years, and a very large 5 year return of about 4x. These moves sit...
